screenshots.rise-onboarding-1.png: Marketing landing page for Articulate Rise 360

observations.onboarding: Rise 360 offers a genuine self-serve 30-day free trial of Articulate 360 — no demo required, no sales call gatekeeping. Users sign up, open Rise in the browser immediately (nothing to install), and can start building a course with the block editor right away. Articulate 360 includes 1,000+ course templates (including 100+ onboarding-specific templates) accessible from the dashboard via a + Create New flow. The community (E-Learning Heroes) provides a dedicated Rise 360 onboarding learning path. The AI 'Create with AI' button on the dashboard offers a guided multi-step course generation flow (topic, audience, tone, objectives). The onboarding experience is widely regarded as low-friction and beginner-friendly. The free trial includes AI features for 30 days; continued AI access requires upgrade to Articulate 360 AI tier.
observations.visual: Rise 360 courses are fully responsive and look polished on any device. Three built-in themes are available: Rise (classic, most flexible), Apex (bold, angular), and Horizon (elegant, understated). Each theme supports customization of cover page layout (8 options), navigation type, lesson headers, accent color (custom hex supported), and font pairing. Custom fonts can be uploaded (brand fonts). A single accent color drives most theme-level branding; reviewers note this single-color constraint limits complex brand palette matching. Content Library 360 (13M+ stock assets) is integrated directly into the editor. AI image generation (editorial, 3D, cartoon, watercolor styles) is available. Course output is clean, modern, and widely praised on G2 as professional-looking. Block layouts are structured and consistent — a strength for rapid production, but a limitation for pixel-level design control.
observations.editor: Rise 360 is an entirely web-based block editor — no desktop install required. Blocks are stacked vertically in a lesson; each block type has its own configuration panel on the right or inline. The editor is WYSIWYG with live preview. Authors select from a block palette (text, media, interactive, knowledge check, divider types). Blocks can be reordered via drag-and-drop, and the new Manage Blocks feature (Q4 2025) allows multi-select reordering and copying across lessons. The Code Block (Q3 2025) allows custom HTML/CSS/JS embeds for fully bespoke interactions. Storyline 360 blocks can be embedded directly, extending Rise's interactivity to anything Storyline can produce. AI Assistant is available throughout the editor for content generation, rewriting, grammar, tone adjustment, quiz generation, image generation, and caption generation. The block-based constraint (no freeform canvas) is the key UX trade-off — extremely fast and predictable, but limited layout flexibility. Real-time collaborative editing is supported for Teams subscribers.
observations.blocks: Rise 360 has one of the richest block libraries in the market. Text blocks: text, statement, quote, numbered/bulleted list. Media blocks: image (multiple layout styles: full-width, text+image, 2–4 column grid, carousel), gallery, audio, video (upload or embed), attachment, embed/iframe. Interactive blocks: accordion, tabs, labeled graphic (hotspot image), process (linear carousel), timeline, scenario (branching roleplay with characters), sorting activity (drag-and-drop categorisation), flashcards, buttons. Knowledge check blocks: multiple choice, multiple response, fill-in-the-blank, matching (unscored, in-lesson). Chart blocks: bar, line, pie (with hover data points). Divider/structural blocks: continue block (gate-progress), divider, spacer. Math equation block (Q1 2025). Code block (Q3 2025, custom HTML/CSS/JS). Table block. Storyline embed block. AI can generate timeline and table blocks from a prompt. Block templates can be saved and shared across Teams accounts.
observations.learner: Published Rise 360 courses are fully responsive — they adapt to any screen size and orientation automatically. The learner experience includes a clean navigation sidebar (customizable: show/hide, menu style), lesson progress indicators (circular fill, checkmarks, or default), and Previous/Next navigation buttons (customizable position, color, can be hidden). Continue blocks allow progressive content reveal, gating interactions. Scenarios support branching roleplay. Sorting activities provide immediate auto-feedback. AI-generated or author-uploaded audio transcripts support accessibility. Captions (AI-generated) are available for video blocks. The Quick Share option lets learners access content via a simple link with light analytics, no LMS required. Reach 360 enables formal assignment-based delivery with progress tracking and completion reporting. Learners can navigate via keyboard. The learner UI is widely praised as modern and visually clean on G2.
observations.publishing: Rise 360 supports a comprehensive range of export and publishing options. LMS export: SCORM 1.2, SCORM 2004, xAPI (Tin Can), cmi5, and AICC. Tracking options: completion percentage, quiz result, or Storyline block completion. PDF export available (noted as not accessible). Web publish (no LMS): Quick Share via trackable link with built-in light analytics. Reach 360: Articulate's own lightweight LMS — direct publish from Rise dashboard, supports assignments, groups, progress tracking, reporting, and manual completion overrides. No native LTI provider/consumer mode confirmed. All publishing is built into the platform — no plugins or third-party tools required. Republishing updated courses does not require re-uploading to the LMS for Reach 360; SCORM packages must be re-uploaded to third-party LMSes.
observations.extensibility: Reach 360 has a REST API and a Webhooks API (events: course.completed, user.created, enrollment events). Rise 360 itself does not have a native authoring REST API — community discussions have raised this as a long-standing gap. SSO: Okta integration is confirmed (OIDC/SWA); broader SSO for author login is described as enterprise-contact-required. Articulate 360 Teams supports SAML-based SSO for enterprise accounts. No native Zapier/Make connector found. No MCP server. Rise integrations page in the Help Center exists but scope is limited. The extensibility story is strong at the LMS/delivery layer (Reach 360 API + webhooks) but weak at the authoring layer (no Rise API).

friction_points: AI features require separate AI tier after trial
journey1_create_course.notes: Sign up for free trial (no demo required) → open Rise dashboard in browser → click + Create New or Create with AI → choose blank course or template → start adding blocks. Very low friction for trial users. AI-guided flow adds outline generation, audience/tone selection before authoring begins.

friction_points: Single accent color limits complex brand matching
No freeform canvas — layout is block-constrained
Storyline embeds require a separate Storyline 360 license and app
journey2_mixed_content.notes: Open lesson → click + Add Block → select block type from palette → configure block (add media, text, interactive items) → preview inline. Mixing text, image, accordion, video, quiz knowledge check, and scenario blocks is straightforward and fast. Block reordering via drag-and-drop. Code block enables arbitrary custom HTML/JS interactions.

friction_points: SCORM packages must be manually re-uploaded to third-party LMSes on update
Reach 360 analytics are basic — not suited for complex enterprise reporting
journey3_publish_share.notes: Click Publish → choose destination (LMS / Reach 360 / Quick Share / PDF / Web) → configure tracking settings and export. For Reach 360, publish directly without exporting a zip. For LMS, download SCORM/xAPI/cmi5/AICC zip and upload to LMS. Quick Share generates a trackable link immediately.
